text.title: API Integration Testing
text.summary: pytest + httpx patterns for integration tests, OpenAPI validation, and auth fixture layout.

rule.statement: Assert on the response body schema, not only the status code. A 200 with a missing required field is a regression.
rule.rationale: Schema drift is the most common API regression. Status code passes; body fails silently without schema assertion.

rule.statement: Generate tests from the served openapi.json (not a hand-maintained spec). Snapshot openapi.json per PR; use oasdiff to classify breaking vs non-breaking changes.
rule.rationale: A served spec is always in sync with the running app. A hand-maintained spec drifts within weeks.

rule.statement: Provide one auth fixture per role: anon_client, user_client, admin_client. Never thread tokens through test arguments.
rule.rationale: Consistent fixture names prevent duplicate auth setup across test files and make role boundaries explicit.

rule.statement: Validate every response against the OpenAPI spec in integration tests. Use openapi-core; reject any test that only checks status_code.
rule.rationale: Status code is a coarse signal. Schema validation catches missing fields, type changes, and unexpected nullability.

example.code:
from openapi_core import OpenAPI from openapi_core.testing.mock import MockRequest, MockResponse
spec = OpenAPI.from_file_path("openapi.json")
def test_response_matches_spec(client): response = client.get("/users") mock_req = MockRequest("http://localhost", "GET", "/users") mock_res = MockResponse(data=response.content, status_code=response.status_code) result = spec.validate_response(mock_req, mock_res) assert not result.errors

rule.statement: For idempotent endpoints, call the operation twice in the same test to catch hidden side-effects.
rule.rationale: A second identical POST must return the same result (201 or 200) not a 500 due to constraint violation.

rule.statement: Require at least one failure-injection test for each dependent service: DB down (mock), third-party 503 (respx/WireMock).
rule.rationale: Happy-path tests pass even when error handling is completely missing. Failure injection surfaces missing try/except and missing fallback responses.
